title: Anthoxanthum clarkei
intro: Anthoxanthum clarkei, also known as Clarke's grass or sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Anthoxanthum clarkei include sesquiterpenoid lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Anthoxanthum clarkei is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
